title: Posting a Teams recap for absent learners
what_it_is: A Teams recap post is a short message in a Teams class channel that sums up what happened in a lesson, so a learner who missed it can read what they need to catch up.
why_use_it: It gives an absent learner a quick, clear way to catch up in their own time, without needing to ask a classmate or wait to speak to you.
how_to: Open Teams and select your class team from the left-hand list.
Select the channel you want to post in, such as General.
Select the New conversation or New post button, usually near the bottom of the screen.
Write a short summary of what the lesson covered — what was taught, and anything the learner needs to do to catch up.
Add any files, links or photos the learner will need, using the paperclip or attachment icon in the post box.
Select Send (the arrow icon) to post the recap to the channel.
Consider using an @mention to tag the specific learner, so they get a notification directly.
